enterpriseDataPlatform.title: Enterprise Industrial Data Platform
enterpriseDataPlatform.summary: Flagship architecture narrative demonstrating how one codebase supports two operating modes: a local reproducible lakehouse for deterministic validation and an enterprise cloud stack for governed production analytics.

executiveSummary.title: Executive Summary
executiveSummary.body: This project presents a production-oriented data platform that keeps engineering rigor visible in a public repository. The architecture separates experimentation from enterprise operation through a two-mode model, while preserving shared contracts, quality assertions, and operational metadata across both modes.

architectureOverview.title: Architecture Overview (Mode 1 / Mode 2)
architectureOverview.intro: The platform intentionally exposes two execution paths so delivery teams can validate logic locally and then promote the same semantics to an enterprise runtime.
architectureOverview.mode1Body: Runs with local orchestration, Delta-based medallion layers, and fixed sample windows. It is optimized for reproducibility, root-cause analysis, and interview-safe demonstration.
architectureOverview.mode2Body: Runs on managed enterprise services with IAM boundaries, centralized catalog/governance, and team-level observability. It is optimized for compliance, scale, and controlled operations.
architectureOverview.tradeoff: Trade-off: Mode 1 prioritizes deterministic debugging and onboarding speed; Mode 2 prioritizes governance controls, security segregation, and long-run reliability. Shared contracts avoid behavior drift between modes.

governance.title: Governance and Data Contracts
governance.item1: Schema contracts are versioned and validated at ingestion boundaries, preventing silent type drift from propagating downstream.
governance.item2: Data quality rules are treated as release gates: failed expectations route records to quarantine and block promotion to trusted layers.
governance.item3: Contract ownership is explicit by layer (Bronze, Silver, Gold), with accountable producers and consumers documented in runbooks/ADRs.
governance.item4: Lineage and run metadata provide auditability for each published dataset, including window, code revision, and validation outcome.

twoModeArchitecture.title: Two-Mode Architecture (Local Lakehouse + Enterprise Stack)
twoModeArchitecture.item1: Mode 1 mirrors medallion semantics locally: Bronze for raw ingestion, Silver for validated conformance, and Gold for analytical consumption.
twoModeArchitecture.item2: Promotion to Mode 2 preserves model contracts and test logic instead of rewriting business rules per environment.
twoModeArchitecture.item3: Enterprise mode introduces managed orchestration, secrets/IAM policies, governed catalogs, and workload isolation by domain/team.
twoModeArchitecture.item4: A consistent run context (run_id, window, validation status) allows deterministic troubleshooting across local and enterprise executions.

observability.title: Observability and Reliability
observability.item1: Pipeline health is tracked through freshness, volume, and quality SLIs with threshold-based alerting tied to operational ownership.
observability.item2: Failure handling is explicit: retries for transient faults, quarantine for invalid data, and replay flow for deterministic recovery.
observability.item3: Dashboards combine platform metrics (latency, throughput) with data-product metrics (contract pass rate, null ratio, drift deltas).
observability.item4: Runbook procedures standardize incident response, reducing MTTR and avoiding ad-hoc recovery steps under pressure.

cicd.title: CI/CD and Quality Gates
cicd.item1: CI validates formatting, static checks, unit tests, and contract-focused transformations before any deployment artifact is promoted.
cicd.item2: Data tests (dbt + expectation suites) run as mandatory gates and fail the pipeline when contract criteria are violated.
cicd.item3: Artifact promotion is stage-based with explicit approvals, preserving traceability from commit to execution window.
cicd.item4: Release discipline prioritizes deterministic outcomes over speed, reducing rollback cost in regulated or high-impact contexts.

adrs.title: Architectural Decisions (ADRs)
adrs.item1: ADR-001: Adopt medallion layering to enforce progressive trust boundaries and simplify downstream consumption contracts.
adrs.item2: ADR-002: Keep a deterministic local mode to shorten feedback loops and guarantee reproducible engineering evidence.
adrs.item3: ADR-003: Encode data quality as executable contracts rather than optional documentation.
adrs.item4: ADR-004: Standardize run metadata and lineage capture to improve auditability and cross-team incident analysis.

reproducibility.title: Reproducibility Commands
reproducibility.intro: The command sequence below is used to bootstrap dependencies, execute deterministic windows, and validate contract gates end-to-end.
reproducibility.note: In enterprise mode, the same logical steps are executed via managed orchestration, preserving contract parity with local runs.

recruiterProof.title: Recruiter Proof Section
recruiterProof.item1: Public repository with executable pipeline paths and operational documentation for independent verification.
recruiterProof.item2: Evidence of architecture reasoning through ADRs, governance boundaries, and quality gate definitions.
recruiterProof.item3: Clear trade-off articulation between deterministic local delivery and enterprise production constraints.
recruiterProof.item4: Measurable artifacts (lineage, quality checks, run metadata) ready for technical interview deep dives.

executiveSummary.title: Executive Summary
executiveSummary.body: Flagship enterprise architecture for telemetry-heavy operations. The platform is event-driven, multi-tenant, and designed for high-throughput ingestion with resilient downstream analytics.

architecture.title: Architecture Highlights
architecture.item1: Kafka-centered event backbone for ingestion, fan-out processing, and replay-safe recovery.
architecture.item2: TimescaleDB and operational read models for low-latency telemetry and trend dashboards.
architecture.item3: Tenant isolation strategy across ingestion, processing, and API boundaries.
architecture.item4: Service interfaces prepared for enterprise integration patterns and future product modules.

reliability.title: Reliability and Observability
reliability.item1: Service-level indicators and alerting strategy for ingestion lag, throughput, and processing failures.
reliability.item2: Structured logging, distributed traces, and metric dashboards for rapid incident triage.
reliability.item3: Failure-mode handling with replay orchestration and deterministic operational recovery.
reliability.item4: HA posture with planned DR fallback for regional degradation scenarios.

security.title: Security Posture
security.item1: Role-based access controls aligned to tenant and operational boundaries.
security.item2: Secret and credential isolation strategy for runtime components and integrations.
security.item3: Event and API contract controls to reduce unauthorized data exposure risk.
security.item4: Audit-oriented operational traces for compliance discussions.

evidence.title: Evidence
evidence.item1: Operational runbooks covering restart, replay, and incident containment procedures.
evidence.item2: Dashboards and alert packs for ingestion health, tenant activity, and latency budgets.
evidence.item3: Documented DR and HA approach with failover assumptions and recovery objectives.

screenshots.title: Screenshot Slots
screenshots.slot1Alt: IoT command and control overview screenshot

roadmap.title: Roadmap to Enterprise-ready
roadmap.body: Next hardening milestones include CI/CD standardization, expanded automated tests, and promotion gates across development, staging, and production flows.

enterpriseDataPlatform.title: Enterprise Industrial Data Platform
enterpriseDataPlatform.summary: Measured outcomes from a two-mode data platform architecture where local deterministic validation and enterprise governed operations share the same contract semantics.
enterpriseDataPlatform.kpisSubtitle: Metrics captured during implementation and hardening cycles to validate platform value and operational readiness.

item1.label: Contract pass rate (Silver -> Gold)
item1.detail: Rows that satisfied mandatory schema and expectation gates before promotion to Gold datasets.

item2.label: Incident triage time
item2.detail: Reduction in average root-cause isolation time after standardized run metadata, alerts, and dashboard baselines.

item3.label: Faster environment onboarding
item3.detail: Acceleration from deterministic Mode 1 bootstrap and reproducible command/runbook execution.

item4.label: Silent schema drift promotions
item4.detail: Schema drifts detected and blocked by contract gates before analytical consumers were exposed.

governance.title: Governance Model
governance.item1: Domain producers own source contracts in Bronze and are accountable for upstream change communication.
governance.item2: Data platform ownership governs shared transformation standards, lineage policies, and release controls.
governance.item3: Data product owners approve Silver-to-Gold semantic changes using versioned contracts and review checklists.
governance.item4: Operational accountability is explicit through run ownership, on-call rotation, and incident postmortem cadence.

quality.title: Data Quality Enforcement
quality.item1: dbt model tests execute as non-optional gates for keys, relationships, and semantic constraints before publication.
quality.item2: Great Expectations checkpoints enforce completeness, validity, and drift boundaries at ingestion and refinement stages.
quality.item3: Failed records are isolated in quarantine datasets with actionable failure metadata for replay and remediation.
quality.item4: Promotion policies require green quality gates in CI/CD, preventing manual bypass in standard release paths.

screenshots.title: Evidence Placeholders
screenshots.subtitle: Slots below are intentionally prepared for public evidence artifacts: dbt lineage, Great Expectations Data Docs, and Mode 2 Grafana monitoring.
screenshots.greatExpectationsAlt: Great Expectations Data Docs screenshot placeholder
screenshots.grafanaAlt: Grafana dashboard screenshot placeholder for Mode 2

outcomes.title: Measured Delivery Outcomes
outcomes.item1: Contract-first release flow reduced downstream rework by catching breaking changes before Gold publication.
outcomes.item2: Governance roles reduced ambiguity in ownership, improving escalation speed during data incidents.
outcomes.item3: Dual-mode architecture preserved developer velocity without compromising enterprise control requirements.
outcomes.item4: Public reproducibility artifacts created interview-grade proof for architecture, reliability, and platform operations.
